   215→def ensure_items_for_all_nodes(tree, ws, image_loader, counters=None):
   216→    """
   217→    Walk tree and create Item masters for ALL nodes (assemblies + leaf items).
   218→    Must run BEFORE Component Master creation since item_code is a Link field.
   219→    Uses ensure_item_exists() from existing BOM Upload module (idempotent).
   220→
   221→    Args:
   222→        tree: List of root nodes
   223→        ws: Excel worksheet
   224→        image_loader: SheetImageLoader instance (or None)
   225→        counters: dict to track {"created": 0, "existing": 0, "failed": 0}
   226→
   227→    Returns:
   228→        dict: counters
   229→    """
   230→    if counters is None:
   231→        counters = {"created": 0, "existing": 0, "failed": 0}
   232→
   233→    for node in tree:
   234→        result = ensure_item_exists(
   235→            node["item_code"],
   236→            node.get("description"),
   237→            node.get("extended_description"),
   238→            node.get("uom"),
   239→            node.get("row_num"),
   240→            ws,
   241→            image_loader,
   242→            node.get("material"),
   243→            node.get("treatment"),
   244→            node.get("weight"),
   245→            node.get("part_number"),
   246→            node.get("manufacturer"),
   247→            node.get("revision")
   248→        )
   249→        counters[result] = counters.get(result, 0) + 1
   250→
   251→        if node.get("children"):
   252→            ensure_items_for_all_nodes(node["children"], ws, image_loader, counters)
   253→
   254→    return counters

   257→# ==================== Step 3: Create Component Masters ====================

   259→def create_component_masters_for_all_items(tree, project):
   260→    """
   261→    Create Component Masters for ALL nodes (assemblies + leaf/RM items).
   262→    For existing CMs: apply make/buy merge logic (Excel wins if present, else keep existing).
   263→    For new CMs: set defaults based on item type.
   264→
   265→    Business Rules:
   266→    - All items default to "Buy" (conservative approach)
   267→    - T, Y, E prefixed items: released_for_procurement = "No"
   268→    - Other items: released_for_procurement = "Yes"
   269→
   270→    Returns:
   271→        dict: {"created": X, "existing": X, "updated": X, "failed": X}
   272→    """
   273→    counters = {"created": 0, "existing": 0, "updated": 0, "failed": 0}
   274→    all_nodes = _get_all_nodes(tree)
   275→
   276→    for node in all_nodes:
   277→        item_code = node["item_code"]
   278→        is_assembly = bool(node.get("children"))
   279→
   280→        # Check if Component Master already exists
   281→        existing = frappe.db.exists(
   282→            "Project Component Master",
   283→            {"project": project, "item_code": item_code},
   284→        )
   285→
   286→        if existing:
   287→            # Merge logic: update make_or_buy only if Excel has a value
   288→            updated = _merge_make_or_buy(existing, node)
   289→            if updated:
   290→                counters["updated"] += 1
   291→            else:
   292→                counters["existing"] += 1
   293→            continue
   294→
   295→        try:
   296→            # Check if item starts with T, Y, or E
   297→            item_code_upper = str(item_code).upper()
   298→            if item_code_upper.startswith(("T", "Y", "E")):
   299→                released_for_procurement = "No"
   300→            else:
   301→                released_for_procurement = "Yes"
   302→
   303→            cm_data = {
   304→                "doctype": "Project Component Master",
   305→                "project": project,
   306→                "item_code": item_code,
   307→                "bom_level": node.get("level", 0),
   308→                "is_loose_item": 0,
   309→                "design_status": "Design Released",
   310→                "project_qty": node.get("qty", 0),  # From Excel column E
   311→                "created_from": "BOM Upload",
   312→                "released_for_procurement": released_for_procurement,
   313→            }
   314→
   315→            if is_assembly:
   316→                cm_data["has_bom"] = 1
   317→                cm_data["active_bom"] = None  # Set later after BOM creation
   318→                cm_data["bom_structure_hash"] = calculate_tree_structure_hash(node["children"])
   319→                # All items default to "Buy" - supply chain team changes to "Make" manually
   320→                cm_data["make_or_buy"] = node.get("make_or_buy") or "Buy"
   321→            else:
   322→                # Leaf item (raw material)
   323→                cm_data["has_bom"] = 0
   324→                cm_data["make_or_buy"] = node.get("make_or_buy") or "Buy"
   325→
   326→            cm = frappe.get_doc(cm_data)
   327→            cm.flags.ignore_validate = True
   328→            cm.flags.ignore_mandatory = True
   329→            cm.insert(ignore_permissions=True)

   331→        except Exception:
   332→            counters["failed"] += 1

   334→                title=f"Failed to create Component Master for {item_code}",

   341→def _merge_make_or_buy(cm_name, node):
   342→    """
   343→    Merge make/buy from Excel into existing Component Master.
   344→    Only updates if Excel has an explicit value; otherwise keeps existing.
   345→
   346→    This allows PE2 team to only tag new items or changes in the Excel.
   347→    Previously tagged items can be left blank.
   348→
   349→    Returns:
   350→        bool: True if value was updated, False if unchanged
   351→    """
   352→    excel_make_or_buy = node.get("make_or_buy")
   353→    if not excel_make_or_buy:
   354→        return False  # No value in Excel, keep existing
   355→
   356→    current_value = frappe.db.get_value(
   357→        "Project Component Master", cm_name, "make_or_buy"
   358→    )
   359→
   360→    if current_value != excel_make_or_buy:
   361→        frappe.db.set_value(
   362→            "Project Component Master", cm_name,
   363→            "make_or_buy", excel_make_or_buy
   364→        )
   365→        return True
   366→    return False

   369→def _get_all_nodes(tree):
   370→    """Flatten tree to get ALL nodes (assemblies + leaf items)."""
   371→    all_nodes = []
   372→    for node in tree:
   373→        all_nodes.append(node)
   374→        if node.get("children"):
   375→            all_nodes.extend(_get_all_nodes(node["children"]))
   376→    return all_nodes

   379→def _get_assembly_nodes(tree):
   380→    """Flatten tree to get all assembly nodes (nodes that have children)."""
   381→    assemblies = []
   382→    for node in tree:
   383→        if node.get("children"):
   384→            assemblies.append(node)
   385→            assemblies.extend(_get_assembly_nodes(node["children"]))

   389→# ==================== Step 4: Analyze Upload ====================

   391→def analyze_upload(tree, project):
   392→    """
   393→    Analyze upload tree for new, unchanged, changed, and blocked components.
   394→
   395→    Returns:
   396→        dict with can_create, changed_components, loose_blocked,
   397→        blocked_by_dependencies, summary
   398→    """
   399→    all_assemblies = _get_assembly_nodes(tree)
   400→    graph = _build_dependency_graph(all_assemblies)
   401→
   402→    categorized = {
   403→        "new": [],
   404→        "unchanged": [],
   405→        "changed": [],
   406→        "loose_blocked": [],
   407→    }
   408→
   409→    for item_code, data in graph.items():
   410→        status, details = _determine_component_status(
   411→            item_code, data["node"], project
   412→        )
   413→        data["status"] = status
   414→        data["details"] = details
   415→        categorized[status].append(item_code)
   416→
   417→    # Find blocking dependencies (ancestor chain)
   418→    changed_set = set(categorized["changed"])
   419→    loose_blocked_set = set(categorized["loose_blocked"])
   420→    blocked_by_changes = _find_blocked_ancestors(
   421→        graph, changed_set, loose_blocked_set
   422→    )
   423→
   424→    # Build list of items safe to create
   425→    blocked_set = set(blocked_by_changes.keys())
   426→    can_create = []
   427→    for item_code in categorized["new"] + categorized["unchanged"]:
   428→        if item_code not in blocked_set:
   429→            can_create.append(graph[item_code]["node"])
   430→
   431→    return {
   432→        "can_create": can_create,
   433→        "changed_components": [graph[ic] for ic in categorized["changed"]],
   434→        "loose_blocked": [graph[ic] for ic in categorized["loose_blocked"]],
   435→        "blocked_by_dependencies": blocked_by_changes,
   436→        "summary": {
   437→            "total": len(all_assemblies),
   438→            "new": len(categorized["new"]),
   439→            "unchanged": len(categorized["unchanged"]),
   440→            "changed": len(categorized["changed"]),
   441→            "loose_blocked": len(categorized["loose_blocked"]),
   442→            "can_create": len(can_create),
   443→            "blocked": len(blocked_by_changes),
   444→        },
   445→    }

   448→def _determine_component_status(item_code, node, project):
   449→    """
   450→    Determine if component is new, unchanged, changed, or loose_blocked.
   451→
   452→    Returns:
   453→        tuple: (status, details)
   454→    """
   455→    component_master = frappe.db.get_value(
   456→        "Project Component Master",
   457→        {"project": project, "item_code": item_code},
   458→        ["name", "is_loose_item", "can_be_converted_to_bom",
   459→         "bom_structure_hash", "active_bom"],
   460→        as_dict=True,
   461→    )
   462→
   463→    if not component_master:
   464→        return "new", {"first_time": True}
   465→
   466→    # Check loose item blocking
   467→    if component_master.is_loose_item and not component_master.can_be_converted_to_bom:
   468→        return "loose_blocked", {
   469→            "component_master": component_master.name,
   470→            "message": f"Loose item {item_code} not enabled for BOM conversion",
   471→        }
   472→
   473→    # Check for BOM changes — no active BOM yet means new BOM needed
   474→    if not component_master.active_bom:
   475→        return "new", {
   476→            "component_master": component_master.name,
   477→            "first_time": False,
   478→        }
   479→
   480→    # Compare BOM structures via hash
   481→    children = node.get("children", [])
   482→    new_hash = calculate_tree_structure_hash(children)
   483→    existing_hash = component_master.bom_structure_hash
   484→
   485→    if new_hash != existing_hash:
   486→        # Check procurement blocking level
   487→        blocking_info = _check_procurement_blocking(
   488→            project, item_code, component_master.active_bom
   489→        )
   490→        return "changed", {
   491→            "change_type": "bom_structure",
   492→            "component_master": component_master.name,
   493→            "old_bom": component_master.active_bom,
   494→            "old_hash": existing_hash,
   495→            "new_hash": new_hash,
   496→            "blocking_level": blocking_info["blocking_level"],
   497→            "blocking_message": blocking_info["message"],
   498→            "can_proceed": blocking_info["can_proceed"],
   499→            "procurement_docs": blocking_info["procurement_docs"],
   500→        }
   501→
   502→    return "unchanged", {
   503→        "component_master": component_master.name,
   504→        "bom": component_master.active_bom,
   505→    }

   508→def _build_dependency_graph(assemblies):
   509→    """
   510→    Build a dependency graph from assembly nodes.
   511→    Maps item_code -> {node, children (item_codes), status, details}
   512→    """
   513→    graph = {}
   514→    for node in assemblies:
   515→        child_codes = [c["item_code"] for c in node.get("children", [])]
   516→        graph[node["item_code"]] = {
   517→            "node": node,
   518→            "children": child_codes,
   519→            "status": None,
   520→            "details": None,
   521→        }
   522→    return graph
   523→

   525→def _find_blocked_ancestors(graph, changed_set, loose_blocked_set):
   526→    """
   527→    Find all components blocked by changed or loose-blocked children.
   528→    Uses iterative approach to propagate blocking up the tree.
   529→
   530→    Returns:
   531→        dict: Mapping of blocked item codes to list of blocking reasons
   532→    """
   533→    blocked = {}
   534→    # Multiple passes to propagate transitive blocking
   535→    changed = True
   536→    while changed:
   537→        changed = False
   538→        for item_code, data in graph.items():
   539→            if item_code in blocked or item_code in changed_set or item_code in loose_blocked_set:
   540→                continue
   541→
   542→            blocking_children = []
   543→            for child_code in data["children"]:
   544→                if child_code in changed_set:
   545→                    blocking_children.append({
   546→                        "item": child_code,
   547→                        "reason": "BOM structure changed",
   548→                    })
   549→                elif child_code in loose_blocked_set:
   550→                    blocking_children.append({
   551→                        "item": child_code,
   552→                        "reason": "Loose item conversion not enabled",
   553→                    })
   554→                elif child_code in blocked:
   555→                    blocking_children.append({
   556→                        "item": child_code,
   557→                        "reason": "Depends on blocked component",
   558→                    })
   559→
   560→            if blocking_children:
   561→                blocked[item_code] = blocking_children
   562→                changed = True
   563→
   564→    return blocked

   641→def _max_blocking_level(current, new_level):
   642→    """Return the stricter blocking level. Order: none < confirm < block < manager_required."""
   643→    levels = {"none": 0, "confirm": 1, "block": 2, "manager_required": 3}
   644→    if levels.get(new_level, 0) > levels.get(current, 0):
   645→        return new_level
   646→    return current

   650→    """Check if current user has manager role for PO-stage override."""

   655→def _check_procurement_blocking(project, item_code, old_bom_name):
   656→    """
   657→    Check if procurement exists and determine blocking level.
   658→
   659→    Checks children of the old BOM for active MR/RFQ/PO.
   660→
   661→    Returns:
   662→        dict: {
   663→            "can_proceed": bool,
   664→            "blocking_level": "none" | "confirm" | "block" | "manager_required",
   665→            "procurement_docs": [...],
   666→            "message": str
   667→        }
   668→    """
   669→    if not old_bom_name:
   670→        return {
   671→            "can_proceed": True,
   672→            "blocking_level": "none",
   673→            "procurement_docs": [],
   674→            "message": "No existing BOM",
   675→        }
   676→
   677→    # Get all child items from old BOM
   678→    old_bom_items = frappe.get_all(
   679→        "BOM Item",
   680→        filters={"parent": old_bom_name},
   681→        fields=["item_code"]
   682→    )
   683→
   684→    blocking_level = "none"
   685→    procurement_docs = []
   686→
   687→    # Check each child item for procurement
   688→    for bom_item in old_bom_items:
   689→        child_item = bom_item.item_code
   690→
   691→        # Check Material Requests
   692→        mrs = _get_material_requests(project, child_item)
   693→        if mrs:
   694→            for mr in mrs:
   695→                procurement_docs.append({
   696→                    "doctype": "Material Request",
   697→                    "name": mr.name,
   698→                    "status": mr.status or "Draft" if mr.docstatus == 0 else "Submitted",
   699→                    "quantity": mr.qty,
   700→                    "item_code": child_item,
   701→                })
   702→            blocking_level = _max_blocking_level(blocking_level, "block")
   703→
   704→        # Check RFQs
   705→        rfqs = _get_rfqs(project, child_item)
   706→        if rfqs:
   707→            for rfq in rfqs:
   708→                procurement_docs.append({
   709→                    "doctype": "Request for Quotation",
   710→                    "name": rfq.name,
   711→                    "status": rfq.status or "Draft" if rfq.docstatus == 0 else "Submitted",
   712→                    "quantity": rfq.qty,
   713→                    "item_code": child_item,
   714→                })
   715→            blocking_level = _max_blocking_level(blocking_level, "block")
   716→
   717→        # Check Purchase Orders
   718→        pos = _get_purchase_orders(project, child_item)
   719→        if pos:
   720→            for po in pos:
   721→                procurement_docs.append({
   722→                    "doctype": "Purchase Order",
   723→                    "name": po.name,
   724→                    "status": po.status or "Draft" if po.docstatus == 0 else "Submitted",
   725→                    "quantity": po.qty,
   726→                    "item_code": child_item,
   727→                })
   728→            blocking_level = _max_blocking_level(blocking_level, "manager_required")
   729→
   730→    # If no procurement found, still need confirmation for hash change
   731→    if blocking_level == "none":
   732→        blocking_level = "confirm"
   733→
   734→    # Determine if user can proceed
   735→    can_proceed = False
   736→    message = ""
   737→
   738→    if blocking_level == "confirm":
   739→        can_proceed = True
   740→        message = "BOM structure changed. Confirmation with remarks required."
   741→    elif blocking_level == "block":
   742→        can_proceed = False
   743→        message = "Cannot change BOM. Child items have active Material Requests or RFQs."
   744→    elif blocking_level == "manager_required":
   745→        if _can_override_po_block():
   746→            can_proceed = True
   747→            message = "Child items have active Purchase Orders. Manager override available."
   748→        else:
   749→            can_proceed = False
   750→            message = "Cannot change BOM. Child items have active Purchase Orders. Manager role required."
   751→
   752→    return {
   753→        "can_proceed": can_proceed,
   754→        "blocking_level": blocking_level,
   755→        "procurement_docs": procurement_docs,
   756→        "message": message,
   757→    }

   801→def _proceed_with_confirmed_changes(doc, confirmed_items):
   802→    """
   803→    Proceed with BOM creation for confirmed changed items.
   804→    Deactivates old BOMs and creates new ones.
   805→    """
   806→    # Parse Excel again
   807→    file_doc = frappe.get_doc("File", {"file_url": doc.bom_file})
   808→    content = file_doc.get_content()
   809→
   810→    wb = openpyxl.load_workbook(io.BytesIO(content), data_only=True)
   811→    ws = wb.active
   812→
   813→    # Initialize image loader
   814→    image_loader = None
   815→    if HAS_IMAGE_LOADER and SheetImageLoader:
   816→        try:
   817→            image_loader = SheetImageLoader(ws)
   818→        except Exception:
   819→            pass
   820→
   821→    rows = parse_rows(ws)
   822→    tree = build_tree(rows)
   823→
   824→    if not tree:
   825→        frappe.throw(_("No components found in the Excel file."))
   826→
   827→    project = doc.project
   828→
   829→    # Ensure items exist
   830→    item_counters = {"created": 0, "existing": 0, "updated": 0, "failed": 0}
   831→    ensure_items_for_all_nodes(tree, ws, image_loader, item_counters)
   832→
   833→    # Deactivate old BOMs for confirmed items
   834→    for item_code in confirmed_items:
   835→        cm = frappe.db.get_value(
   836→            "Project Component Master",
   837→            {"project": project, "item_code": item_code},
   838→            ["name", "active_bom"],
   839→            as_dict=True
   840→        )
   841→        if cm and cm.active_bom:
   842→            # Deactivate the old BOM
   843→            old_bom = frappe.get_doc("BOM", cm.active_bom)
   844→            old_bom.is_active = 0
   845→            old_bom.is_default = 0
   846→            old_bom.flags.ignore_validate = True
   847→            old_bom.save(ignore_permissions=True)
   848→
   849→            # Clear active_bom so new one can be linked
   850→            frappe.db.set_value(
   851→                "Project Component Master",
   852→                cm.name,
   853→                "active_bom",
   854→                None
   855→            )
   856→
   857→    # Build analysis with confirmed items treated as "new"
   858→    all_assemblies = _get_assembly_nodes(tree)
   859→    confirmed_set = set(confirmed_items)
   860→
   861→    can_create = []
   862→    for node in all_assemblies:
   863→        item_code = node["item_code"]
   864→
   865→        # Check if already has active BOM (unchanged)
   866→        cm = frappe.db.get_value(
   867→            "Project Component Master",
   868→            {"project": project, "item_code": item_code},
   869→            ["active_bom", "bom_structure_hash"],
   870→            as_dict=True
   871→        )
   872→
   873→        if cm and cm.active_bom:
   874→            # Already has active BOM - compare hashes
   875→            children = node.get("children", [])
   876→            new_hash = calculate_tree_structure_hash(children)
   877→            if new_hash == cm.bom_structure_hash:
   878→                continue  # Unchanged, skip
   879→
   880→        # Either new, confirmed, or needs BOM
   881→        can_create.append(node)
   882→
   883→    # Create BOMs
   884→    analysis = {
   885→        "can_create": can_create,
   886→        "changed_components": [],
   887→        "loose_blocked": [],
   888→        "blocked_by_dependencies": {},
   889→        "summary": {"can_create": len(can_create)},
   890→    }
   891→
   892→    result = create_boms_and_link_components(tree, project, analysis, ws, image_loader)
   893→
   894→    frappe.db.commit()
   895→    return result

   898→# ==================== Step 6 & 7: Create BOMs and Link ====================
   899→
   900→def create_boms_and_link_components(tree, project, analysis, ws, image_loader):
   901→    """
   902→    Create BOMs for components that passed analysis, then link active_bom
   903→    back to their Component Masters.
   904→
   905→    Args:
   906→        tree: List of root nodes
   907→        project: Project name
   908→        analysis: Analysis results dict
   909→        ws: Excel worksheet
   910→        image_loader: SheetImageLoader instance (or None)
   911→
   912→    Returns:
   913→        dict: {"status": "success", "created": X, "skipped": X, "failed": X, "errors": []}
   914→    """
   915→    created = 0
   916→    skipped = 0
   917→    failed = 0
   918→    errors = []
   919→
   920→    # Only create BOMs for items in the can_create list
   921→    can_create_codes = {n["item_code"] for n in analysis["can_create"]}
   922→
   923→    for node in tree:
   924→        try:
   925→            c, s, f, e = _create_bom_for_node(node, project, can_create_codes, ws, image_loader)
   926→            created += c
   927→            skipped += s
   928→            failed += f
   929→            errors.extend(e)
   930→        except Exception as ex:
   931→            failed += 1
   932→            errors.append(f"{node['item_code']}: {str(ex)}")
   933→            frappe.log_error(
   934→                title=f"BOM creation failed for {node['item_code']}",
   935→                message=frappe.get_traceback(),
   936→            )
   937→
   938→    # Link active_bom back to Component Masters
   939→    _link_boms_to_component_masters(project)
   940→
   941→    return {
   942→        "status": "success",
   943→        "created": created,
   944→        "skipped": skipped,
   945→        "failed": failed,
   946→        "errors": errors,
   947→    }
   948→
   949→
   950→def _create_bom_for_node(node, project, can_create_codes, ws, image_loader):
   951→    """
   952→    Create BOM for a single node using existing create_bom_recursive.
   953→    Only processes nodes whose item_code is in can_create_codes.
   954→
   955→    Args:
   956→        node: Tree node dict
   957→        project: Project name
   958→        can_create_codes: Set of item codes allowed to create BOMs
   959→        ws: Excel worksheet
   960→        image_loader: SheetImageLoader instance (or None)
   961→
   962→    Returns:
   963→        tuple: (created, skipped, failed, errors)
   964→    """
   965→    created = 0
   966→    skipped = 0
   967→    failed = 0
   968→    errors = []
   969→
   970→    item_code = node["item_code"]
   971→
   972→    if not node.get("children"):
   973→        # Leaf node — no BOM needed
   974→        return created, skipped, failed, errors
   975→
   976→    if item_code not in can_create_codes:
   977→        skipped += 1
   978→        return created, skipped, failed, errors
   979→
   980→    try:
   981→        created_flag = create_bom_recursive(node, project, ws, image_loader)
   982→        if created_flag:
   983→            created += 1
   984→        else:
   985→            skipped += 1
   986→    except Exception as ex:
   987→        failed += 1
   988→        errors.append(f"{item_code}: {str(ex)}")
   989→
   990→    # Recurse into children
   991→    for child in node.get("children", []):
   992→        if child.get("children"):
   993→            c, s, f, e = _create_bom_for_node(child, project, can_create_codes, ws, image_loader)
   994→            created += c
   995→            skipped += s
   996→            failed += f
   997→            errors.extend(e)
   998→
   999→    return created, skipped, failed, errors

  1002→def _link_boms_to_component_masters(project):
  1003→    """
  1004→    After BOMs are created, link active_bom back to Component Masters
  1005→    that don't have one set yet.
  1006→    """
  1007→    # Find Component Masters without active_bom
  1008→    cms = frappe.get_all(
  1009→        "Project Component Master",
  1010→        filters={
  1011→            "project": project,
  1012→            "has_bom": 1,
  1013→            "active_bom": ("is", "not set"),
  1014→        },
  1015→        fields=["name", "item_code"],
  1016→    )
  1017→
  1018→    for cm_data in cms:
  1019→        # Find the active default BOM for this item
  1020→        bom_name = frappe.db.get_value(
  1021→            "BOM",
  1022→            {
  1023→                "item": cm_data.item_code,
  1024→                "project": project,
  1025→                "is_active": 1,
  1026→                "is_default": 1,
  1027→                "docstatus": 1,
  1028→            },
  1029→            "name",
  1030→        )
  1031→
  1032→        if not bom_name:
  1033→            # Also check without project filter (BOM may not have project set)
  1034→            bom_name = frappe.db.get_value(
  1035→                "BOM",
  1036→                {
  1037→                    "item": cm_data.item_code,
  1038→                    "is_active": 1,
  1039→                    "is_default": 1,
  1040→                    "docstatus": 1,
  1041→                },
  1042→                "name",
  1043→            )
  1044→
  1045→        if bom_name:
  1046→            cm = frappe.get_doc("Project Component Master", cm_data.name)
  1047→            cm.active_bom = bom_name
  1048→
  1049→            # Recalculate hash from actual BOM doc
  1050→            try:
  1051→                bom_doc = frappe.get_doc("BOM", bom_name)
  1052→                cm.bom_structure_hash = calculate_bom_doc_hash(bom_doc)
  1053→            except Exception:
  1054→                pass
  1055→
  1056→            cm.flags.ignore_validate = True
  1057→            cm.flags.ignore_mandatory = True
  1058→            cm.save(ignore_permissions=True)

  1061→# ==================== Hash Calculation ====================
  1062→
  1063→def calculate_tree_structure_hash(children):
  1064→    """
  1065→    Create hash of BOM structure from tree node children (parsed from Excel).
  1066→    Uses same logic as bom_hooks.calculate_bom_structure_hash but works
  1067→    on tree node dicts instead of BOM doc items.
  1068→
  1069→    Args:
  1070→        children: List of child node dicts with item_code and qty
  1071→
  1072→    Returns:
  1073→        str: MD5 hash of sorted (item_code, qty) tuples
  1074→    """
  1075→    if not children:
  1076→        return None
  1077→
  1078→    structure = sorted(
  1079→        [(child["item_code"], float(child.get("qty", 1))) for child in children],
  1080→        key=lambda x: x[0],
  1081→    )
  1082→    structure_str = json.dumps(structure, sort_keys=True)
  1083→    return hashlib.md5(structure_str.encode()).hexdigest()
  1084→

  1086→# ==================== Summary Message ====================
  1087→
  1088→def _build_summary_message(summary, errors=None):
  1089→    """
  1090→    Build a human-readable summary message.
  1091→
  1092→    Args:
  1093→        summary: dict with items, boms, component_masters sections
  1094→        errors: list of error strings
  1095→
  1096→    Returns:
  1097→        str: Formatted summary message
  1098→    """
  1099→    parts = []
  1100→
  1101→    items = summary.get("items", {})
  1102→    if items.get("created"):
  1103→        parts.append(f"{items['created']} Items created")
  1104→    if items.get("existing"):
  1105→        parts.append(f"{items['existing']} Items already existed")
  1106→
  1107→    boms = summary.get("boms", {})
  1108→    if boms.get("created"):
  1109→        parts.append(f"{boms['created']} BOMs created")
  1110→    if boms.get("existing"):
  1111→        parts.append(f"{boms['existing']} BOMs already existed")
  1112→
  1113→    cms = summary.get("component_masters", {})
  1114→    if cms.get("created"):
  1115→        parts.append(f"{cms['created']} Component Masters created")
  1116→    if cms.get("existing"):
  1117→        parts.append(f"{cms['existing']} Component Masters already existed")
  1118→    if cms.get("updated"):
  1119→        parts.append(f"{cms['updated']} Component Masters updated")
  1120→
  1121→    # Add failure warnings
  1122→    failures = items.get("failed", 0) + boms.get("failed", 0) + cms.get("failed", 0)
  1123→    if failures:
  1124→        parts.append(f"⚠️ {failures} failures")
  1125→
  1126→    if errors and len(errors) > 0:
  1127→        parts.append(f"⚠️ {len(errors)} errors (check error log)")
  1128→
  1129→    return ". ".join(parts) + "." if parts else "No changes made."

  1133→
  1134→def _serialize_analysis(analysis):

"""
Enhanced BOM Upload with Validation and Component Master Integration

New "Create BOMs with Validation" button on BOM Upload form.
Imports existing functions from bom_upload.py — does NOT modify that file.

Sequence (Decision 11 & 12):
  1. Parse Excel (reuse parse_rows, build_tree)
  2. Create Items for assemblies (reuse ensure_item_exists)
  3. Create Component Masters FIRST (active_bom=null)
  4. Analyze upload — hash comparison, blocking checks
  5. Create BOMs bottom-up (reuse create_bom_recursive)
  6. Link active_bom back to Component Masters
  7. BOM on_submit hooks auto-populate BOM Usage tables
"""

import frappe
from frappe import _
import openpyxl
import io
import hashlib
import json

from clevertech.clevertech.doctype.bom_upload.bom_upload import (
    parse_rows,
    build_tree,
    ensure_item_exists,
    create_bom_recursive,
    HAS_IMAGE_LOADER,
)

# Conditional import for image loader (same as bom_upload.py)
try:
    from openpyxl_image_loader import SheetImageLoader
except ImportError:
    SheetImageLoader = None

from clevertech.project_component_master.bom_hooks import (
    calculate_bom_structure_hash as calculate_bom_doc_hash,
)


# ==================== Main Entry Point ====================

@frappe.whitelist()
def create_boms_with_validation(docname):
    """
    Enhanced BOM creation with validation and Component Master integration.

    Args:
        docname: BOM Upload document name

    Returns:
        dict: Analysis results or creation summary
    """
    doc = frappe.get_doc("BOM Upload", docname)

    if not doc.bom_file:
        frappe.throw(_("Please attach a BOM Excel file first."))

    if not doc.project:
        frappe.throw(_("Please select a Project first."))

    # Step 1: Parse Excel (reuse existing logic)
    file_doc = frappe.get_doc("File", {"file_url": doc.bom_file})
    content = file_doc.get_content()

    wb = openpyxl.load_workbook(io.BytesIO(content), data_only=True)
    ws = wb.active

    # Initialize image loader only if available
    image_loader = None
    if HAS_IMAGE_LOADER and SheetImageLoader:
        try:
            image_loader = SheetImageLoader(ws)
        except Exception:
            pass  # Image loading optional

    rows = parse_rows(ws)
    tree = build_tree(rows)

    if not tree:
        frappe.throw(_("No components found in the Excel file."))

    # Step 2: Create Items for ALL nodes (assemblies + leaf items)
    # Items must exist BEFORE Component Masters (item_code is a Link field to Item)
    item_counters = {"created": 0, "existing": 0, "updated": 0, "failed": 0}
    ensure_items_for_all_nodes(tree, ws, image_loader, item_counters)

    # Step 3: Create Component Masters FIRST for ALL items (assemblies + raw materials)
    # All items default to "Buy" (conservative). T, Y, E items get released_for_procurement=No.
    # (active_bom=null at this stage for assemblies, set later after BOM creation)
    cm_counters = create_component_masters_for_all_items(tree, doc.project)

    # Step 4: Analyze upload (now Component Masters exist for blocking checks)
    analysis = analyze_upload(tree, doc.project)

    # Step 5: Check for blocking issues
    if analysis["loose_blocked"]:
        return {
            "status": "blocked",
            "reason": "loose_items_not_enabled",
            "analysis": _serialize_analysis(analysis),
            "message": _("Enable 'Can be converted to BOM' for loose items first"),
        }

    if analysis["changed_components"]:
        # Categorize changed components by blocking level
        hard_blocked = []  # MR/RFQ blocks - user must deactivate old BOM
        manager_blocked = []  # PO blocks - need manager role
        confirmable = []  # No procurement - just need confirmation

        for comp in analysis["changed_components"]:
            details = comp.get("details", {})
            blocking_level = details.get("blocking_level", "confirm")

            if blocking_level == "manager_required":
                if details.get("can_proceed"):
                    # User has manager role, can confirm
                    confirmable.append(comp)
                else:
                    # User lacks manager role
                    manager_blocked.append(comp)
            elif blocking_level == "block":
                hard_blocked.append(comp)
            else:
                # confirm level
                confirmable.append(comp)

        if hard_blocked:
            return {
                "status": "procurement_blocked",
                "reason": "active_mr_rfq",
                "blocked_components": [
                    {
                        "item_code": c["node"]["item_code"],
                        "details": c.get("details", {}),
                    }
                    for c in hard_blocked
                ],
                "analysis": _serialize_analysis(analysis),
                "message": _("Cannot change BOM. Deactivate old BOMs manually, then re-run upload."),
            }

        if manager_blocked:
            return {
                "status": "manager_required",
                "reason": "active_po_no_role",
                "blocked_components": [
                    {
                        "item_code": c["node"]["item_code"],
                        "details": c.get("details", {}),
                    }
                    for c in manager_blocked
                ],
                "analysis": _serialize_analysis(analysis),
                "message": _("Cannot change BOM. Child items have active POs. Manager role required."),
            }

        if confirmable:
            return {
                "status": "requires_confirmation",
                "confirmable_components": [
                    {
                        "item_code": c["node"]["item_code"],
                        "details": c.get("details", {}),
                    }
                    for c in confirmable
                ],
                "analysis": _serialize_analysis(analysis),
                "message": _("BOM structure changed. Confirm with remarks to proceed."),
            }

    # Step 6 & 7: Create BOMs and link back to Component Masters
    result = create_boms_and_link_components(tree, doc.project, analysis, ws, image_loader)

    # Build comprehensive summary
    result["summary"] = {
        "items": {
            "created": item_counters["created"],
            "existing": item_counters["existing"],
            "updated": item_counters["updated"],
            "failed": item_counters["failed"],
            "total": sum(item_counters.values())
        },
        "boms": {
            "created": result.pop("created", 0),
            "existing": result.pop("skipped", 0),
            "failed": result.pop("failed", 0),
            "total": 0  # Will be calculated below
        },
        "component_masters": {
            "created": cm_counters["created"],
            "existing": cm_counters["existing"],
            "updated": cm_counters["updated"],
            "failed": cm_counters["failed"],
            "total": sum(cm_counters.values())
        }
    }
    result["summary"]["boms"]["total"] = (
        result["summary"]["boms"]["created"] +
        result["summary"]["boms"]["existing"] +
        result["summary"]["boms"]["failed"]
    )

    # Build user-friendly message
    result["message"] = _build_summary_message(result["summary"], result.get("errors", []))

    frappe.db.commit()
    return result


# ==================== Step 2: Ensure Items ====================

def ensure_items_for_all_nodes(tree, ws, image_loader, counters=None):
    """
    Walk tree and create Item masters for ALL nodes (assemblies + leaf items).
    Must run BEFORE Component Master creation since item_code is a Link field.
    Uses ensure_item_exists() from existing BOM Upload module (idempotent).

    Args:
        tree: List of root nodes
        ws: Excel worksheet
        image_loader: SheetImageLoader instance (or None)
        counters: dict to track {"created": 0, "existing": 0, "failed": 0}

    Returns:
        dict: counters
    """
    if counters is None:
        counters = {"created": 0, "existing": 0, "failed": 0}

    for node in tree:
        result = ensure_item_exists(
            node["item_code"],
            node.get("description"),
            node.get("extended_description"),
            node.get("uom"),
            node.get("row_num"),
            ws,
            image_loader,
            node.get("material"),
            node.get("treatment"),
            node.get("weight"),
            node.get("part_number"),
            node.get("manufacturer"),
            node.get("revision")
        )
        counters[result] = counters.get(result, 0) + 1

        if node.get("children"):
            ensure_items_for_all_nodes(node["children"], ws, image_loader, counters)

    return counters


# ==================== Step 3: Create Component Masters ====================

def create_component_masters_for_all_items(tree, project):
    """
    Create Component Masters for ALL nodes (assemblies + leaf/RM items).
    For existing CMs: apply make/buy merge logic (Excel wins if present, else keep existing).
    For new CMs: set defaults based on item type.

    Business Rules:
    - All items default to "Buy" (conservative approach)
    - T, Y, E prefixed items: released_for_procurement = "No"
    - Other items: released_for_procurement = "Yes"

    Returns:
        dict: {"created": X, "existing": X, "updated": X, "failed": X}
    """
    counters = {"created": 0, "existing": 0, "updated": 0, "failed": 0}
    all_nodes = _get_all_nodes(tree)

    for node in all_nodes:
        item_code = node["item_code"]
        is_assembly = bool(node.get("children"))

        # Check if Component Master already exists
        existing = frappe.db.exists(
            "Project Component Master",
            {"project": project, "item_code": item_code},
        )

        if existing:
            # Merge logic: update make_or_buy only if Excel has a value
            updated = _merge_make_or_buy(existing, node)
            if updated:
                counters["updated"] += 1
            else:
                counters["existing"] += 1
            continue

        try:
            # Check if item starts with T, Y, or E
            item_code_upper = str(item_code).upper()
            if item_code_upper.startswith(("T", "Y", "E")):
                released_for_procurement = "No"
            else:
                released_for_procurement = "Yes"

            cm_data = {
                "doctype": "Project Component Master",
                "project": project,
                "item_code": item_code,
                "bom_level": node.get("level", 0),
                "is_loose_item": 0,
                "design_status": "Design Released",
                "project_qty": node.get("qty", 0),  # From Excel column E
                "created_from": "BOM Upload",
                "released_for_procurement": released_for_procurement,
            }

            if is_assembly:
                cm_data["has_bom"] = 1
                cm_data["active_bom"] = None  # Set later after BOM creation
                cm_data["bom_structure_hash"] = calculate_tree_structure_hash(node["children"])
                # All items default to "Buy" - supply chain team changes to "Make" manually
                cm_data["make_or_buy"] = node.get("make_or_buy") or "Buy"
            else:
                # Leaf item (raw material)
                cm_data["has_bom"] = 0
                cm_data["make_or_buy"] = node.get("make_or_buy") or "Buy"

            cm = frappe.get_doc(cm_data)
            cm.flags.ignore_validate = True
            cm.flags.ignore_mandatory = True
            cm.insert(ignore_permissions=True)
            counters["created"] += 1
        except Exception:
            counters["failed"] += 1
            frappe.log_error(
                title=f"Failed to create Component Master for {item_code}",
                message=frappe.get_traceback(),
            )

    return counters


def _merge_make_or_buy(cm_name, node):
    """
    Merge make/buy from Excel into existing Component Master.
    Only updates if Excel has an explicit value; otherwise keeps existing.

    This allows PE2 team to only tag new items or changes in the Excel.
    Previously tagged items can be left blank.

    Returns:
        bool: True if value was updated, False if unchanged
    """
    excel_make_or_buy = node.get("make_or_buy")
    if not excel_make_or_buy:
        return False  # No value in Excel, keep existing

    current_value = frappe.db.get_value(
        "Project Component Master", cm_name, "make_or_buy"
    )

    if current_value != excel_make_or_buy:
        frappe.db.set_value(
            "Project Component Master", cm_name,
            "make_or_buy", excel_make_or_buy
        )
        return True
    return False


def _get_all_nodes(tree):
    """Flatten tree to get ALL nodes (assemblies + leaf items)."""
    all_nodes = []
    for node in tree:
        all_nodes.append(node)
        if node.get("children"):
            all_nodes.extend(_get_all_nodes(node["children"]))
    return all_nodes


def _get_assembly_nodes(tree):
    """Flatten tree to get all assembly nodes (nodes that have children)."""
    assemblies = []
    for node in tree:
        if node.get("children"):
            assemblies.append(node)
            assemblies.extend(_get_assembly_nodes(node["children"]))
    return assemblies


# ==================== Step 4: Analyze Upload ====================

def analyze_upload(tree, project):
    """
    Analyze upload tree for new, unchanged, changed, and blocked components.

    Returns:
        dict with can_create, changed_components, loose_blocked,
        blocked_by_dependencies, summary
    """
    all_assemblies = _get_assembly_nodes(tree)
    graph = _build_dependency_graph(all_assemblies)

    categorized = {
        "new": [],
        "unchanged": [],
        "changed": [],
        "loose_blocked": [],
    }

    for item_code, data in graph.items():
        status, details = _determine_component_status(
            item_code, data["node"], project
        )
        data["status"] = status
        data["details"] = details
        categorized[status].append(item_code)

    # Find blocking dependencies (ancestor chain)
    changed_set = set(categorized["changed"])
    loose_blocked_set = set(categorized["loose_blocked"])
    blocked_by_changes = _find_blocked_ancestors(
        graph, changed_set, loose_blocked_set
    )

    # Build list of items safe to create
    blocked_set = set(blocked_by_changes.keys())
    can_create = []
    for item_code in categorized["new"] + categorized["unchanged"]:
        if item_code not in blocked_set:
            can_create.append(graph[item_code]["node"])

    return {
        "can_create": can_create,
        "changed_components": [graph[ic] for ic in categorized["changed"]],
        "loose_blocked": [graph[ic] for ic in categorized["loose_blocked"]],
        "blocked_by_dependencies": blocked_by_changes,
        "summary": {
            "total": len(all_assemblies),
            "new": len(categorized["new"]),
            "unchanged": len(categorized["unchanged"]),
            "changed": len(categorized["changed"]),
            "loose_blocked": len(categorized["loose_blocked"]),
            "can_create": len(can_create),
            "blocked": len(blocked_by_changes),
        },
    }


def _determine_component_status(item_code, node, project):
    """
    Determine if component is new, unchanged, changed, or loose_blocked.

    Returns:
        tuple: (status, details)
    """
    component_master = frappe.db.get_value(
        "Project Component Master",
        {"project": project, "item_code": item_code},
        ["name", "is_loose_item", "can_be_converted_to_bom",
         "bom_structure_hash", "active_bom"],
        as_dict=True,
    )

    if not component_master:
        return "new", {"first_time": True}

    # Check loose item blocking
    if component_master.is_loose_item and not component_master.can_be_converted_to_bom:
        return "loose_blocked", {
            "component_master": component_master.name,
            "message": f"Loose item {item_code} not enabled for BOM conversion",
        }

    # Check for BOM changes — no active BOM yet means new BOM needed
    if not component_master.active_bom:
        return "new", {
            "component_master": component_master.name,
            "first_time": False,
        }

    # Compare BOM structures via hash
    children = node.get("children", [])
    new_hash = calculate_tree_structure_hash(children)
    existing_hash = component_master.bom_structure_hash

    if new_hash != existing_hash:
        # Check procurement blocking level
        blocking_info = _check_procurement_blocking(
            project, item_code, component_master.active_bom
        )
        return "changed", {
            "change_type": "bom_structure",
            "component_master": component_master.name,
            "old_bom": component_master.active_bom,
            "old_hash": existing_hash,
            "new_hash": new_hash,
            "blocking_level": blocking_info["blocking_level"],
            "blocking_message": blocking_info["message"],
            "can_proceed": blocking_info["can_proceed"],
            "procurement_docs": blocking_info["procurement_docs"],
        }

    return "unchanged", {
        "component_master": component_master.name,
        "bom": component_master.active_bom,
    }


def _build_dependency_graph(assemblies):
    """
    Build a dependency graph from assembly nodes.
    Maps item_code -> {node, children (item_codes), status, details}
    """
    graph = {}
    for node in assemblies:
        child_codes = [c["item_code"] for c in node.get("children", [])]
        graph[node["item_code"]] = {
            "node": node,
            "children": child_codes,
            "status": None,
            "details": None,
        }
    return graph


def _find_blocked_ancestors(graph, changed_set, loose_blocked_set):
    """
    Find all components blocked by changed or loose-blocked children.
    Uses iterative approach to propagate blocking up the tree.

    Returns:
        dict: Mapping of blocked item codes to list of blocking reasons
    """
    blocked = {}
    # Multiple passes to propagate transitive blocking
    changed = True
    while changed:
        changed = False
        for item_code, data in graph.items():
            if item_code in blocked or item_code in changed_set or item_code in loose_blocked_set:
                continue

            blocking_children = []
            for child_code in data["children"]:
                if child_code in changed_set:
                    blocking_children.append({
                        "item": child_code,
                        "reason": "BOM structure changed",
                    })
                elif child_code in loose_blocked_set:
                    blocking_children.append({
                        "item": child_code,
                        "reason": "Loose item conversion not enabled",
                    })
                elif child_code in blocked:
                    blocking_children.append({
                        "item": child_code,
                        "reason": "Depends on blocked component",
                    })

            if blocking_children:
                blocked[item_code] = blocking_children
                changed = True

    return blocked


def _get_procurement_documents(project, item_code):
    """
    Get active procurement documents for a component.
    Returns list of dicts with doctype, name, status.
    """
    cm_name = frappe.db.get_value(
        "Project Component Master",
        {"project": project, "item_code": item_code},
        "name",
    )
    if not cm_name:
        return []

    records = frappe.get_all(
        "Component Procurement Record",
        filters={"parent": cm_name},
        fields=["document_type", "document_name", "status", "quantity"],
    )

    return [
        {
            "doctype": r.document_type,
            "name": r.document_name,
            "status": r.status or "Unknown",
            "quantity": r.quantity,
        }
        for r in records
    ]


# ==================== Phase 4E: Procurement Blocking ====================

def _get_material_requests(project, item_code):
    """Get active (non-cancelled) Material Requests for the item in the project."""
    return frappe.db.sql("""
        SELECT
            mr.name, mr.docstatus, mr.status,
            mri.qty
        FROM `tabMaterial Request` mr
        INNER JOIN `tabMaterial Request Item` mri ON mri.parent = mr.name
        WHERE mri.item_code = %(item_code)s
        AND mr.custom_project_ = %(project)s
        AND mr.docstatus != 2
    """, {"item_code": item_code, "project": project}, as_dict=True)


def _get_rfqs(project, item_code):
    """Get active (non-cancelled) RFQs for the item in the project."""
    return frappe.db.sql("""
        SELECT
            rfq.name, rfq.docstatus, rfq.status,
            rfqi.qty
        FROM `tabRequest for Quotation` rfq
        INNER JOIN `tabRequest for Quotation Item` rfqi ON rfqi.parent = rfq.name
        WHERE rfqi.item_code = %(item_code)s
        AND rfq.custom_project = %(project)s
        AND rfq.docstatus != 2
    """, {"item_code": item_code, "project": project}, as_dict=True)


def _get_purchase_orders(project, item_code):
    """Get active (non-cancelled) Purchase Orders for the item in the project."""
    return frappe.db.sql("""
        SELECT
            po.name, po.docstatus, po.status,
            poi.qty
        FROM `tabPurchase Order` po
        INNER JOIN `tabPurchase Order Item` poi ON poi.parent = po.name
        WHERE poi.item_code = %(item_code)s
        AND po.project = %(project)s
        AND po.docstatus != 2
    """, {"item_code": item_code, "project": project}, as_dict=True)


def _max_blocking_level(current, new_level):
    """Return the stricter blocking level. Order: none < confirm < block < manager_required."""
    levels = {"none": 0, "confirm": 1, "block": 2, "manager_required": 3}
    if levels.get(new_level, 0) > levels.get(current, 0):
        return new_level
    return current


def _can_override_po_block():
    """Check if current user has manager role for PO-stage override."""
    user_roles = frappe.get_roles()
    return "Component Master Manager" in user_roles or "System Manager" in user_roles


def _check_procurement_blocking(project, item_code, old_bom_name):
    """
    Check if procurement exists and determine blocking level.

    Checks children of the old BOM for active MR/RFQ/PO.

    Returns:
        dict: {
            "can_proceed": bool,
            "blocking_level": "none" | "confirm" | "block" | "manager_required",
            "procurement_docs": [...],
            "message": str
        }
    """
    if not old_bom_name:
        return {
            "can_proceed": True,
            "blocking_level": "none",
            "procurement_docs": [],
            "message": "No existing BOM",
        }

    # Get all child items from old BOM
    old_bom_items = frappe.get_all(
        "BOM Item",
        filters={"parent": old_bom_name},
        fields=["item_code"]
    )

    blocking_level = "none"
    procurement_docs = []

    # Check each child item for procurement
    for bom_item in old_bom_items:
        child_item = bom_item.item_code

        # Check Material Requests
        mrs = _get_material_requests(project, child_item)
        if mrs:
            for mr in mrs:
                procurement_docs.append({
                    "doctype": "Material Request",
                    "name": mr.name,
                    "status": mr.status or "Draft" if mr.docstatus == 0 else "Submitted",
                    "quantity": mr.qty,
                    "item_code": child_item,
                })
            blocking_level = _max_blocking_level(blocking_level, "block")

        # Check RFQs
        rfqs = _get_rfqs(project, child_item)
        if rfqs:
            for rfq in rfqs:
                procurement_docs.append({
                    "doctype": "Request for Quotation",
                    "name": rfq.name,
                    "status": rfq.status or "Draft" if rfq.docstatus == 0 else "Submitted",
                    "quantity": rfq.qty,
                    "item_code": child_item,
                })
            blocking_level = _max_blocking_level(blocking_level, "block")

        # Check Purchase Orders
        pos = _get_purchase_orders(project, child_item)
        if pos:
            for po in pos:
                procurement_docs.append({
                    "doctype": "Purchase Order",
                    "name": po.name,
                    "status": po.status or "Draft" if po.docstatus == 0 else "Submitted",
                    "quantity": po.qty,
                    "item_code": child_item,
                })
            blocking_level = _max_blocking_level(blocking_level, "manager_required")

    # If no procurement found, still need confirmation for hash change
    if blocking_level == "none":
        blocking_level = "confirm"

    # Determine if user can proceed
    can_proceed = False
    message = ""

    if blocking_level == "confirm":
        can_proceed = True
        message = "BOM structure changed. Confirmation with remarks required."
    elif blocking_level == "block":
        can_proceed = False
        message = "Cannot change BOM. Child items have active Material Requests or RFQs."
    elif blocking_level == "manager_required":
        if _can_override_po_block():
            can_proceed = True
            message = "Child items have active Purchase Orders. Manager override available."
        else:
            can_proceed = False
            message = "Cannot change BOM. Child items have active Purchase Orders. Manager role required."

    return {
        "can_proceed": can_proceed,
        "blocking_level": blocking_level,
        "procurement_docs": procurement_docs,
        "message": message,
    }


@frappe.whitelist()
def confirm_version_change(docname, confirmations):
    """
    Confirm BOM version changes and proceed with upload.

    Args:
        docname: BOM Upload document name
        confirmations: JSON string of list of dicts with item_code and remarks

    Returns:
        dict: Creation summary
    """
    if isinstance(confirmations, str):
        confirmations = json.loads(confirmations)

    doc = frappe.get_doc("BOM Upload", docname)
    project = doc.project

    # Store remarks in Component Masters
    for conf in confirmations:
        item_code = conf.get("item_code")
        remarks = conf.get("remarks", "")

        cm_name = frappe.db.get_value(
            "Project Component Master",
            {"project": project, "item_code": item_code},
            "name"
        )

        if cm_name:
            frappe.db.set_value(
                "Project Component Master",
                cm_name,
                "version_change_remarks",
                remarks
            )

    # Re-run the upload, now allowing changed components
    return _proceed_with_confirmed_changes(doc, [c["item_code"] for c in confirmations])


def _proceed_with_confirmed_changes(doc, confirmed_items):
    """
    Proceed with BOM creation for confirmed changed items.
    Deactivates old BOMs and creates new ones.
    """
    # Parse Excel again
    file_doc = frappe.get_doc("File", {"file_url": doc.bom_file})
    content = file_doc.get_content()

    wb = openpyxl.load_workbook(io.BytesIO(content), data_only=True)
    ws = wb.active

    # Initialize image loader
    image_loader = None
    if HAS_IMAGE_LOADER and SheetImageLoader:
        try:
            image_loader = SheetImageLoader(ws)
        except Exception:
            pass

    rows = parse_rows(ws)
    tree = build_tree(rows)

    if not tree:
        frappe.throw(_("No components found in the Excel file."))

    project = doc.project

    # Ensure items exist
    item_counters = {"created": 0, "existing": 0, "updated": 0, "failed": 0}
    ensure_items_for_all_nodes(tree, ws, image_loader, item_counters)

    # Deactivate old BOMs for confirmed items
    for item_code in confirmed_items:
        cm = frappe.db.get_value(
            "Project Component Master",
            {"project": project, "item_code": item_code},
            ["name", "active_bom"],
            as_dict=True
        )
        if cm and cm.active_bom:
            # Deactivate the old BOM
            old_bom = frappe.get_doc("BOM", cm.active_bom)
            old_bom.is_active = 0
            old_bom.is_default = 0
            old_bom.flags.ignore_validate = True
            old_bom.save(ignore_permissions=True)

            # Clear active_bom so new one can be linked
            frappe.db.set_value(
                "Project Component Master",
                cm.name,
                "active_bom",
                None
            )

    # Build analysis with confirmed items treated as "new"
    all_assemblies = _get_assembly_nodes(tree)
    confirmed_set = set(confirmed_items)

    can_create = []
    for node in all_assemblies:
        item_code = node["item_code"]

        # Check if already has active BOM (unchanged)
        cm = frappe.db.get_value(
            "Project Component Master",
            {"project": project, "item_code": item_code},
            ["active_bom", "bom_structure_hash"],
            as_dict=True
        )

        if cm and cm.active_bom:
            # Already has active BOM - compare hashes
            children = node.get("children", [])
            new_hash = calculate_tree_structure_hash(children)
            if new_hash == cm.bom_structure_hash:
                continue  # Unchanged, skip

        # Either new, confirmed, or needs BOM
        can_create.append(node)

    # Create BOMs
    analysis = {
        "can_create": can_create,
        "changed_components": [],
        "loose_blocked": [],
        "blocked_by_dependencies": {},
        "summary": {"can_create": len(can_create)},
    }

    result = create_boms_and_link_components(tree, project, analysis, ws, image_loader)

    frappe.db.commit()
    return result


# ==================== Step 6 & 7: Create BOMs and Link ====================

def create_boms_and_link_components(tree, project, analysis, ws, image_loader):
    """
    Create BOMs for components that passed analysis, then link active_bom
    back to their Component Masters.

    Args:
        tree: List of root nodes
        project: Project name
        analysis: Analysis results dict
        ws: Excel worksheet
        image_loader: SheetImageLoader instance (or None)

    Returns:
        dict: {"status": "success", "created": X, "skipped": X, "failed": X, "errors": []}
    """
    created = 0
    skipped = 0
    failed = 0
    errors = []

    # Only create BOMs for items in the can_create list
    can_create_codes = {n["item_code"] for n in analysis["can_create"]}

    for node in tree:
        try:
            c, s, f, e = _create_bom_for_node(node, project, can_create_codes, ws, image_loader)
            created += c
            skipped += s
            failed += f
            errors.extend(e)
        except Exception as ex:
            failed += 1
            errors.append(f"{node['item_code']}: {str(ex)}")
            frappe.log_error(
                title=f"BOM creation failed for {node['item_code']}",
                message=frappe.get_traceback(),
            )

    # Link active_bom back to Component Masters
    _link_boms_to_component_masters(project)

    return {
        "status": "success",
        "created": created,
        "skipped": skipped,
        "failed": failed,
        "errors": errors,
    }


def _create_bom_for_node(node, project, can_create_codes, ws, image_loader):
    """
    Create BOM for a single node using existing create_bom_recursive.
    Only processes nodes whose item_code is in can_create_codes.

    Args:
        node: Tree node dict
        project: Project name
        can_create_codes: Set of item codes allowed to create BOMs
        ws: Excel worksheet
        image_loader: SheetImageLoader instance (or None)

    Returns:
        tuple: (created, skipped, failed, errors)
    """
    created = 0
    skipped = 0
    failed = 0
    errors = []

    item_code = node["item_code"]

    if not node.get("children"):
        # Leaf node — no BOM needed
        return created, skipped, failed, errors

    if item_code not in can_create_codes:
        skipped += 1
        return created, skipped, failed, errors

    try:
        created_flag = create_bom_recursive(node, project, ws, image_loader)
        if created_flag:
            created += 1
        else:
            skipped += 1
    except Exception as ex:
        failed += 1
        errors.append(f"{item_code}: {str(ex)}")

    # Recurse into children
    for child in node.get("children", []):
        if child.get("children"):
            c, s, f, e = _create_bom_for_node(child, project, can_create_codes, ws, image_loader)
            created += c
            skipped += s
            failed += f
            errors.extend(e)

    return created, skipped, failed, errors


def _link_boms_to_component_masters(project):
    """
    After BOMs are created, link active_bom back to Component Masters
    that don't have one set yet.
    """
    # Find Component Masters without active_bom
    cms = frappe.get_all(
        "Project Component Master",
        filters={
            "project": project,
            "has_bom": 1,
            "active_bom": ("is", "not set"),
        },
        fields=["name", "item_code"],
    )

    for cm_data in cms:
        # Find the active default BOM for this item
        bom_name = frappe.db.get_value(
            "BOM",
            {
                "item": cm_data.item_code,
                "project": project,
                "is_active": 1,
                "is_default": 1,
                "docstatus": 1,
            },
            "name",
        )

        if not bom_name:
            # Also check without project filter (BOM may not have project set)
            bom_name = frappe.db.get_value(
                "BOM",
                {
                    "item": cm_data.item_code,
                    "is_active": 1,
                    "is_default": 1,
                    "docstatus": 1,
                },
                "name",
            )

        if bom_name:
            cm = frappe.get_doc("Project Component Master", cm_data.name)
            cm.active_bom = bom_name

            # Recalculate hash from actual BOM doc
            try:
                bom_doc = frappe.get_doc("BOM", bom_name)
                cm.bom_structure_hash = calculate_bom_doc_hash(bom_doc)
            except Exception:
                pass

            cm.flags.ignore_validate = True
            cm.flags.ignore_mandatory = True
            cm.save(ignore_permissions=True)


# ==================== Hash Calculation ====================

def calculate_tree_structure_hash(children):
    """
    Create hash of BOM structure from tree node children (parsed from Excel).
    Uses same logic as bom_hooks.calculate_bom_structure_hash but works
    on tree node dicts instead of BOM doc items.

    Args:
        children: List of child node dicts with item_code and qty

    Returns:
        str: MD5 hash of sorted (item_code, qty) tuples
    """
    if not children:
        return None

    structure = sorted(
        [(child["item_code"], float(child.get("qty", 1))) for child in children],
        key=lambda x: x[0],
    )
    structure_str = json.dumps(structure, sort_keys=True)
    return hashlib.md5(structure_str.encode()).hexdigest()


# ==================== Summary Message ====================

def _build_summary_message(summary, errors=None):
    """
    Build a human-readable summary message.

    Args:
        summary: dict with items, boms, component_masters sections
        errors: list of error strings

    Returns:
        str: Formatted summary message
    """
    parts = []

    items = summary.get("items", {})
    if items.get("created"):
        parts.append(f"{items['created']} Items created")
    if items.get("existing"):
        parts.append(f"{items['existing']} Items already existed")

    boms = summary.get("boms", {})
    if boms.get("created"):
        parts.append(f"{boms['created']} BOMs created")
    if boms.get("existing"):
        parts.append(f"{boms['existing']} BOMs already existed")

    cms = summary.get("component_masters", {})
    if cms.get("created"):
        parts.append(f"{cms['created']} Component Masters created")
    if cms.get("existing"):
        parts.append(f"{cms['existing']} Component Masters already existed")
    if cms.get("updated"):
        parts.append(f"{cms['updated']} Component Masters updated")

    # Add failure warnings
    failures = items.get("failed", 0) + boms.get("failed", 0) + cms.get("failed", 0)
    if failures:
        parts.append(f"⚠️ {failures} failures")

    if errors and len(errors) > 0:
        parts.append(f"⚠️ {len(errors)} errors (check error log)")

    return ". ".join(parts) + "." if parts else "No changes made."


# ==================== Serialization ====================

def _serialize_analysis(analysis):
    """
    Serialize analysis dict for JSON response to client.
    Convert node dicts to safe serializable format.
    """
    def serialize_node(node):
        return {
            "item_code": node.get("item_code"),
            "description": node.get("description"),
            "level": node.get("level"),
            "qty": node.get("qty"),
        }

    def serialize_details(details):
        """Serialize details dict, ensuring procurement_docs is serializable."""
        if not details:
            return {}
        return {
            "change_type": details.get("change_type"),
            "component_master": details.get("component_master"),
            "old_bom": details.get("old_bom"),
            "blocking_level": details.get("blocking_level"),
            "blocking_message": details.get("blocking_message"),
            "can_proceed": details.get("can_proceed"),
            "procurement_docs": details.get("procurement_docs", []),
        }

    serialized = {
        "can_create": [serialize_node(n) for n in analysis.get("can_create", [])],
        "changed_components": [],
        "loose_blocked": [],
        "blocked_by_dependencies": analysis.get("blocked_by_dependencies", {}),
        "summary": analysis.get("summary", {}),
    }

    for comp in analysis.get("changed_components", []):
        serialized["changed_components"].append({
            "node": serialize_node(comp.get("node", {})),
            "details": serialize_details(comp.get("details", {})),
        })

    for comp in analysis.get("loose_blocked", []):
        serialized["loose_blocked"].append({
            "node": serialize_node(comp.get("node", {})),
            "details": comp.get("details", {}),
        })

    return serialized

   513→# ================= Parse Excel =================
   514→
   515→def parse_rows(ws):
   516→
   517→    rows = []
   518→
   519→    for r in range(3, ws.max_row + 1):
   520→
   521→        code_raw = ws[f"C{r}"].value
   522→        if not code_raw:
   523→            continue
   524→
   525→        position = ws[f"A{r}"].value or ws[f"B{r}"].value
   526→
   527→        rows.append({
   528→            "row_num": r,  # Store row number for image extraction
   529→            "position": position,
   530→            "item_code": clean_code(code_raw),
   531→            "description": ws[f"D{r}"].value,
   532→            "extended_description": ws[f"U{r}"].value,  # DESCRIZIONE_ESTESA for item description
   533→            "qty": to_float(ws[f"E{r}"].value, 1),
   534→            "revision": ws[f"G{r}"].value,
   535→            "material": ws[f"AD{r}"].value,
   536→            "part_number": ws[f"AE{r}"].value,
   537→            "weight": to_float(ws[f"AF{r}"].value, 0),
   538→            "manufacturer": ws[f"AG{r}"].value,
   539→            "treatment": ws[f"AL{r}"].value,
   540→            "uom": normalize_uom(ws[f"AN{r}"].value),
   541→            "level": int(ws[f"AR{r}"].value or 0),
   542→            "children": []
   543→        })
   544→
   545→    return rows
   546→
   547→
   548→# ================= Hierarchy =================
   549→
   550→def build_tree(rows):
   551→
   552→    stack = []
   553→    roots = []
   554→
   555→    for row in rows:
   556→
   557→        while stack and stack[-1]["level"] >= row["level"]:
   558→            stack.pop()
   559→        if stack:
   560→            stack[-1]["children"].append(row)
   561→        else:
   562→            roots.append(row)
   563→
   564→        stack.append(row)
   565→
   566→    return roots

   571→def ensure_item_exists(item_code, description, extended_description, uom, row_num, ws, image_loader, material=None, treatment=None, weight=None, part_number=None, manufacturer=None, revision=None):
   572→    """
   573→    Create Item master if it doesn't exist.
   574→    If item exists but has no image, update it with image from Excel.
   575→
   576→    Returns:
   577→        str: "created" | "existing" | "updated" | "failed"
   578→    """
   579→    if frappe.db.exists("Item", item_code):
   580→        # Item exists - check if we need to update image
   581→        existing_item = frappe.get_doc("Item", item_code)
   582→
   583→        # Only update image if item doesn't have one and Excel has an image
   584→        if not existing_item.image and HAS_IMAGE_LOADER and image_loader:
   585→            try:
   586→                image_cell = f"A{row_num}"
   587→                if image_loader.image_in(image_cell):
   588→                    img = image_loader.get(image_cell)
   589→                    img_bytes = io.BytesIO()
   590→                    img.save(img_bytes, format="PNG")
   591→
   592→                    file_obj = save_file(
   593→                        f"{item_code}.png",
   594→                        img_bytes.getvalue(),
   595→                        "Item",
   596→                        item_code,
   597→                        is_private=0
   598→                    )
   599→
   600→                    existing_item.image = file_obj.file_url
   601→                    existing_item.flags.ignore_validate = True
   602→                    existing_item.save(ignore_permissions=True)
   603→                    return "updated"
   604→            except Exception as e:
   605→                frappe.log_error(f"Image update failed for {item_code}: {str(e)}", "BOM Upload Image Error")
   606→
   607→        return "existing"
   608→
   609→    item_name = description or item_code
   610→    item_description = extended_description or description or item_code
   611→    
   612→    # Get item group and HSN code based on item code prefix
   613→    item_group, hsn_code = get_item_group_and_hsn(item_code)
   614→    
   615→    # Get default expense account based on item code prefix
   616→    default_expense_account = get_default_expense_account(item_code)
   617→    
   618→    # Normalize material
   619→    normalized_material = normalize_material(material)

   621→    # Get type of material from description
   622→    type_of_material = get_type_of_material(description)

   625→    surface_treatment = get_surface_treatment(treatment)
   626→
   627→    item = frappe.get_doc({

   675→

import frappe
from frappe.model.document import Document
from frappe.utils.file_manager import save_file
import openpyxl
import io

# Conditional import for image loader
try:
    from openpyxl_image_loader import SheetImageLoader
    HAS_IMAGE_LOADER = True
except ImportError:
    HAS_IMAGE_LOADER = False
    frappe.log_error("openpyxl_image_loader not installed. Image upload will be skipped.", "BOM Upload")


class BOMUpload(Document):
    pass


@frappe.whitelist()
def create_boms(docname):
    doc = frappe.get_doc("BOM Upload", docname)
    return _create_boms(doc)


def _create_boms(doc):

    file_doc = frappe.get_doc("File", {"file_url": doc.bom_file})
    content = file_doc.get_content()

    wb = openpyxl.load_workbook(io.BytesIO(content), data_only=True)
    ws = wb.active
    
    # Initialize image loader only if available
    image_loader = SheetImageLoader(ws) if HAS_IMAGE_LOADER else None

    rows = parse_rows(ws)
    tree = build_tree(rows)

    created = 0
    skipped = 0
    failed = 0
    errors = []

    for node in tree:
        try:
            created_flag = create_bom_recursive(node, doc.project, ws, image_loader)
            if created_flag:
                created += 1
            else:
                skipped += 1
        except Exception as e:
            failed += 1
            errors.append(f"{node['item_code']} → {str(e)}")

    return {
        "created": created,
        "skipped": skipped,
        "failed": failed,
        "errors": errors
    }


# ================= Excel helpers =================

def clean_code(code):
    if not code:
        return None
    return str(code).replace(".", "").strip()


def to_float(val, default=0):
    try:
        return float(val)
    except Exception:
        return default


def normalize_uom(uom):
    if not uom:
        return "Nos"
    
    # Convert to string and clean
    u = str(uom).strip().upper()
    
    # If it's a number or doesn't match known UOMs, default to Nos
    if u.isdigit():
        return "Nos"
    
    mapping = {
        "NUMERI": "Nos",
        "PEZZI": "PIECES",
        "METRI": "Meter",
        "MQ": "Sqm",  # Metro Quadro (Square Meter)
        "PACKAGES": "Nos"
    }
    
    # Return mapped value, or default to Nos if not found
    return mapping.get(u, "Nos")
